author: terencechain
content: <@586161934425128960> dont you guys send payload attestation asap as well? so if the incoming block is not head, you just dont send it? or you would still send it at the deadline (just not asap)

author: nc1234
content: Yea we send asap. In the latest version of Lodestar, we don't send anything anymore if the incoming block is not head.

author: nc1234
content: We have PR drafts to either [send ptc vote for any block ](https://github.com/ChainSafe/lodestar/pull/9461) or [just do first seen](https://github.com/ChainSafe/lodestar/pull/9453) but we haven't come into conclusion what the correct behaviour is

author: nflaig
content: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/892088344438255616/1514374732215095418 also related to the discussion here <@358120958726373381> <@755590043632140352>, we cannot do a ptc attestation for **any** block, it at least needs to be compatible with the head views shuffling, otherwise you gonna produce a ptc attestation which will be rejected on gossip because the validator is not part of the ptc committee, ie. this check fails `[REJECT] The message's validator index is within the payload committee in get_ptc(state, data.slot)`

author: potuz
content: Yeah but that reject needs to be an ignore if people are not using the state of the blockroot of the PTC attestation!

author: nflaig
content: that a bug in the implementation then

author: potuz
content: The target state of the attester is what matters not the receiver

author: potuz
content: I'm guessing that lighthouse downscored us incorrectly if we sent a payload attestation for a different view than their head, otherwise we have a bug

author: potuz
content: Anyway better to have this argument in one place instead of two

author: nflaig
content: that's a lighthouse bug then and separate from what I mean, let's discuss that in interop channel

author: nflaig
content: but my point above is something else, but could also trigger the same condition but be the fault of the attester
